data.description:
# Docs: private-repo credentials, descriptive errors, ref/source semantics, HMAC trust model

## Why

The snapshot model adds an operational requirement the old "read the local checkout" model
did not have — the server must be able to `fetch` from origin, which for private repos means
credentials. Operators and agents need clear guidance and clear failures.

## What

- Update the MCP-server setup docs (the "stand up a rebar MCP server" instructions +
  jira-sync-setup-style references) to state the PRIVATE-REPO FETCH CREDENTIAL requirement:
  the server's clone needs a credential helper / deploy key / token to fetch arbitrary refs.
- Document the descriptive credential-failure behavior (what the actionable error looks like
  and how to fix it), and that attested mode fails closed while local mode still runs.
- Document the `ref` + `source` semantics: attested (default, pinned SHA, signs, records
  `verified_at_sha`) vs local (in-place checkout, never signs); the `origin/main` default.
- Document the HMAC trust model + limits (integrity + intra-domain authenticity only; no
  non-repudiation / public verifiability / transparency) and the in-toto Statement shape.
- Note `REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR`, the disk-cap behavior, and the EFS/NFS flock caveat (cross-ref
  alto-fruit-punch).

## Scope

- In: setup-doc credential section, error-behavior docs, ref/source semantics, HMAC trust-model
  doc, the new env/knobs.
- Out: code (S1-S5).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The MCP setup docs state the private-repo fetch-credential requirement and how to supply it.
- [ ] The descriptive missing-credential error is documented (message + remedy); fail-closed vs
      local-still-runs behavior is stated.
- [ ] `ref`/`source` semantics + the `origin/main` default are documented for CLI and MCP.
- [ ] The HMAC trust model + limits and the in-toto Statement shape are documented.
- [ ] `REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR`, disk-cap, and the EFS/NFS flock caveat are documented (xref alto-fruit-punch).
data.title: Docs: private-repo credentials, descriptive errors, ref/source semantics, HMAC trust model
